proven.claim: Preconditions establishing a safe logging situation
proven.smt2:
; Preconditions establishing a safe logging situation
(declare-const err_is_null Bool)
(declare-const err_has_message Bool)
(declare-const err_is_string Bool)        ; true ⇔ err (the object itself) is a string

; Establish that we are in the safe regime:
; 1. err is NOT null/undefined.
; 2. err.message is a defined (non‑empty) string.
; 3. If we ever fall back to `err`, then `err` is also a string.
(assert (not err_is_null))
(assert err_has_message)
(assert (=> (and (not err_has_message) (not err_is_null)) err_is_string))

; Negated property: the logged value is NOT a string.
; We model the logged value as a boolean `log_is_string`.
(declare-const log_is_string Bool)
(assert (not log_is_string))

; Relate `log_is_string` to the concrete flags:
;   - If err_has_message then the log is a string (the message).
;   - Otherwise (fallback) the log is a string exactly when err_is_string.
(assert (= log_is_string (or err_has_message err_is_string)))

; The conjunction of the above constraints is contradictory:
(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat → under the stated preconditions the log is
; guaranteed to be a string, so the claim is proven.

violations.smt2:
(declare-const err_is_null Bool)        ; err is null/undefined
(declare-const err_has_message Bool)    ; err.message is a defined non‑empty string

; -------------------- code transition --------------------
; The catch‑handler does NOT test `err` before using it, so the
; state after the exception can be any combination of the two flags.
; No additional constraints are added here (i.e., both flags are
; completely unconstrained by the surrounding code).

; -------------------- violation condition --------------------
; Violation: either err is null (property access would throw) OR
; err is non‑null but err.message is falsy (fallback to `err` may be non‑string).
(assert (or err_is_null (and (not err_is_null) (not err_has_message))))

(check-sat)
; Expected result: sat → the unsafe situation is reachable because the
; handler never validates `err` before accessing its `message` property.
